---
title: "Week 3 - capstone"
format: html
editor: visual
---

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(babynames)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggrepel)

tracked_names <- tibble::tibble(
  name = c("Mary", "Jennifer", "Lisa", "Emma", "James", "Michael", "John", "Aiden"),
  sex  = c("F",    "F",        "F",   "F",   "M",     "M",       "M",    "M")
)

bn_20c <- babynames %>%
  filter(year >= 1900, year <= 2000)

totals <- bn_20c %>%
  group_by(year, sex) %>%
  summarise(total_births_sex_year = sum(n), .groups = "drop")

ts <- bn_20c %>%
  semi_join(tracked_names, by = c("name", "sex")) %>%
  select(year, sex, name, n) %>%
  left_join(totals, by = c("year", "sex")) %>%
  mutate(
    share = n / total_births_sex_year,
    share_pct = 100 * share
  ) %>%
  arrange(sex, name, year)

# Peak table (this is where peak_share_pct is created)
peak_table <- ts %>%
  group_by(name, sex) %>%
  summarise(
    peak_year = year[which.max(share_pct)],
    peak_share_pct = max(share_pct),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(peak_share_pct))

peak_table

# Labels for the last year (2000)
labels_df <- ts %>%
  group_by(sex, name) %>%
  slice_max(order_by = year, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup()

ggplot(ts, aes(x = year, y = share_pct, color = name)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  facet_wrap(
    ~ sex, ncol = 1, scales = "free_y",
    labeller = labeller(sex = c(F = "Female names", M = "Male names"))
  ) +
  geom_text_repel(
    data = labels_df,
    aes(label = name),
    direction = "y",
    hjust = 0,
    nudge_x = 2,
    segment.alpha = 0.4,
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(1900, 2005)) +
  labs(
    title = "Baby Name Popularity as Social Trend (USA, 1900–2000)",
    subtitle = "Share of births within each sex-year (denominator uses ALL names)",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Share of births within sex (%)",
    color = "Name",
    caption = "Data: babynames. share = n / total births in the same year and sex."
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
```
